html:
> They are in the process of building a new country little by little.

Mass immigration also builds a new country little by little. As just one example, election results would look very different if the US still had 1960s, or even 1980s demographics. Per the New York Times:

Not long ago, this rolling green stretch of Northern Virginia was farmland. Most people who could vote had grown up here. And when they did, they usually chose Republicans. [..] Unlike three decades ago, the residents are often from other places, like India and Korea. And when they vote, it is often for Democrats. - How Voters Turned Virginia From Deep Red to Solid Blue, https://www.nytimes.com/2019/11/09/us/virginia-elections-dem... (https://archive.is/Vwll8)

It's more accurate to say the new country has already been built, and they are trying to restore the old one.
